meta.name: Deserialization
meta.description: Insecure deserialization payloads and detection techniques for PHP, Java, Python pickle, Node.js, and Ruby. Covers gadget chain indicators, detection probes, and tool references.

payloads.description: PHP benign serialized object — safe probe to confirm PHP unserialize() is being called
PHP serialized User object — probe with known class name; watch for class not found errors
Laravel gadget chain entry — triggers __destruct chain in vulnerable Laravel versions
Guzzle PHP gadget chain base64 — example of serialized gadget chain payload structure
Java serialized payload marker — starts with rO0AB (base64 of aced0005), confirms Java deserial
Java serialization magic bytes hex — every Java serialized stream starts with 0xaced 0x0005
ysoserial CommonsCollections1 gadget — generates RCE payload for Apache Commons Collections
ysoserial Spring gadget chain — RCE via Spring Framework deserialization
ysoserial URLDNS gadget — safe DNS-only probe; no RCE, only confirms deserialization
Python pickle RCE payload generator — __reduce__ method executes OS command on unpickle
Python pickle opcode RCE — raw pickle opcodes calling os.system('id')

Python pickle base64 payload decoding — decodes to cos
system
(S'id'
tR.
Node.js node-serialize RCE — IIFE function in serialized JSON triggers RCE on deserialize
node-serialize JSON RCE payload — JSON with IIFE marker for node-serialize library
Ruby Marshal.load RCE probe — Marshal format string; full RCE requires gadget chain
Ruby Marshal magic bytes — 0x04 0x08 indicates Ruby Marshal serialized data
Java serialization content-type header — endpoint accepts serialized Java objects
ASP.NET ViewState with MAC disabled — attempt tamper if EnableViewStateMac=false
